perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the "Confidential Information" of the Disclosing Party shall mean all information concerning or related to the business, operations, financial condition or prospects of the Disclosing Party or any of their respective Affiliates, regardless of the form in which such information appears and whether or not such information has been reduced to a tangible form, and shall specifically include (1) all information regarding the officers, directors, employees, equity holders, customers, suppliers, distributors, insurers, reinsurers, brokers, independent contractors, sales representatives and licensees of the Disclosing Party and their respective Affiliates, in each case whether present or prospective, (2) all inventions, discoveries, trade secrets, processes, techniques, methods, formulae, ideas and know-how of the Disclosing Party and their respective Affiliates, (3) all financial statements, audit reports, budgets and business plans or forecasts of the Disclosing Party and their respective Affiliates and (4) all information concerning or related to the Transaction; provided, that the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party shall not include (x) information which is or becomes generally known to the public through no act or omission of the Receiving Party and (y) information which has been or hereafter is lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a source other than the Disclosing Party (or any of their respective Affiliates or their
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the "Confidential Information" of the Disclosing Party shall mean all data concerning the business, operations, or financial health of the Disclosing Party, regardless of its format, and shall encompass (1) details about officers, directors, personnel, shareholders, clients, providers, vendors, insurers, reinsurers, agents, independent workers, salespeople, and permittees of the Disclosing Party, both current and future, (2) all innovations, explorations, proprietary data, workflows, methodologies, equations, concepts, and expertise of the Disclosing Party, (3) all fiscal records, audit reports, estimates, and strategic layouts of the Disclosing Party, and (4) details pertaining to the Transaction; provided, that the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party shall exclude (x) details generally accessible without Receiver's action or inaction and (y) details legally sourced by the Receiver from outside channels, unaffiliated with the Disclosing Party or their
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'Confidential Information' to different terms such as replacing 'information' with 'data' and 'personnel' with 'employees', and 'clients' with 'customers', and 'providers' with 'suppliers' and then 'Receiver' with 'Receiving Party' introduces ambiguity and contradiction. While aiming to cover similar ground, the new terms may be interpreted differently, narrowing or broadening the scope of confidential information. This contradicts the intent in later sections where the original definition is implied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. If activity in respect of the Transaction shall cease without the Transaction being consummated, then, promptly after the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information which is in tangible form and which is then in its possession (or in the possession of any of its officers, directors or employees).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data', 'employees' to 'personnel', 'customers' to 'clients', 'suppliers' to 'providers', and 'Receiving Party' to 'Receiver' in the definition of 'Confidential Information' creates a strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information', defined with the original terminology. By changing these terms, the modified definition introduces ambiguity. Now, it is unclear if 'data', 'personnel', 'clients', 'providers', and actions by the 'Receiver' are intended to be treated the same way as 'information', 'employees', 'customers', 'suppliers', and the 'Receiving Party' in the 'Return of Confidential Information' clause. This ambiguity directly impacts the Receiving Party's obligation to return information, potentially leading to disputes over what constitutes 'Confidential Information' that must be returned. The original definition is implied in the contradicted section and the new definition is misaligned, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: As used in this Paragraph, an "Affiliate" of a Disclosing Party shall mean an entity which controls, is controlled by or is under common control of a Disclosing Party, and the term "control" shall mean, with respect to any entity, the possession, direct or indirect, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of such entity, whether through ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Paragraph, an "Affiliate" of a Disclosing Party shall mean an entity which controls, is influenced by or is under common management of a Disclosing Party, and the term "control" shall mean, with respect to any entity, the possession, direct or indirect, of the power to guide or cause the guiding of the leadership and strategies of such entity, whether through ownership of voting securities, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'controlled by' to 'influenced by', 'common control' to 'common management', 'direct' to 'guide', 'direction of the management and policies' to 'guiding of the leadership and strategies' modifies the scope and nature of what constitutes an affiliate and 'control'. This introduces a contradiction, as later sections rely on the original, stricter definition of 'control' and 'affiliate' when assigning responsibilities and liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Equitable Relief. The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that the Disclosing Party and GreenWay would be irreparably damaged in the event that any of the provisions of this Agreement are not performed by the Receiving Party in accordance with their specific terms or are otherwise breached. Accordingly, it is agreed that the Disclosing Party or GreenWay shall be entitled to an injunction or injunctions to prevent breaches of this Agreement by the Receiving Party and shall have the right to specifically enforce this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof against the Receiving Party in addition to any other remedy to which the Disclosing Party or GreenWay may be entitled in law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Affiliate' and weakens the definition of 'control' by using terms like 'influenced by', 'common management', 'guide', and 'guiding of the leadership and strategies'. The original text relies on a stricter definition of these terms. This creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' grants equitable relief to the Disclosing Party and GreenWay based on the original understanding of 'Affiliate' and 'control'. With the modified definitions, it becomes unclear whether the same level of protection and enforcement applies, as the scope of who is considered an 'Affiliate' and what constitutes 'control' has been altered. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' as the basis for 'Equitable Relief' is now uncertain, leading to a potential enforcement issue.

perturbation.original_text: Either Parties' failure to insist in any one or more instances upon strict performance by the other party of any of the terms of this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of any continuing or subsequent failure to perform or a delay in performance of any term hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: If one party doesn't always enforce every detail of this Agreement, it doesn't mean they've given up the right to enforce it later.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Either Parties' failure to insist in any one or more instances upon strict performance by the other party of any of the terms of this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of any continuing or subsequent failure to perform or a delay in performance of any term hereof.' to 'If one party doesn't always enforce every detail of this Agreement, it doesn't mean they've given up the right to enforce it later.' replaces precise legal wording with a more colloquial phrase. While attempting to convey the same idea of non-waiver, the new language lacks the legal precision, potentially leading to interpretations where 'every detail' might be construed differently than 'any of the terms hereof.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes and contains the complete and final integrated agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter herein. All prior negotiations, discussions and representations are merged into this Agreement. Each Party acknowledges that, except as expressly set forth herein, no representations of any kind or character have been made to it by any other party, or by any party's agents, representatives or attorneys, to induce the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains precise legal wording regarding waiver. The changed text replaces this with a colloquial paraphrase. While intending to convey the same meaning, the vagueness of 'every detail' in the changed text introduces ambiguity. This directly contradicts the 'Entire Agreement' clause, which stipulates that the agreement is complete and final, and that no representations outside the agreement are valid. The less precise language in the changed text opens the door to interpretations and arguments not supported by the original legally precise text, thereby contradicting the intent and enforceability of the 'Entire Agreement' clause.
